nameMeanings.name: Wallis
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from Anglo-Norman French Waleis, Walais ‘Welshman’, also sometimes ‘Breton’ (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning a ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). In western and central England the medieval reference is clearly to Welshmen. In Norfolk and Lincolnshire, the reference was probably to Bretons, many of whom settled in the eastern counties after the Conquest. Compare Welsh , Walsh . The idea that the Scottish surname refers to the Welsh-speaking Britons of Strathclyde is erroneous.

variants.name: Wallace
meanings: Scottish and English: variant of   Wallis  , especially in Scotland, where the name was introduced from the Welsh Marches by a family of tenants of the Stewarts in the 12th century.
Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish (Ashkenazic) surnames, e.g.   Wallach  .

variants.name: Wales
meanings: habitational name from Wales near Sheffield (South Yorkshire). The placename means ‘The Welshmen’ and denoted a settlement of Brittonic speakers living on the boundary between the Anglo-Saxon kingdoms of Northumbria and Mercia (from Old English wēalas, walas, plural of walh, wealh, originally meaning ‘Roman citizen’ and referring to the native British population, but in Old English this term later came to mean ‘serf, unfree person, foreigner’ or ‘Welshman’). See also Wallis .
habitational name from Wales, the country named from Old English wēalas ‘foreigners’ (especially those speaking Celtic or Romance languages). This meaning of the surname is quite rare.

variants.name: Welsh
meanings: Scottish, English, and Irish: ethnic name from Middle English welshe (Old English wēalisc, wēlisc) ‘foreign, Welsh’, for someone from Wales or a speaker of the Welsh language. Compare Walsh and Wallace .
Americanized form of Ukrainian and Jewish (from Ukraine) Voloshin or its Polish variant Wołoszyn (see Woloszyn ).

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis was born in 1225, in Sullington, Sussex, England as the daughter of Richard le Waleys. She married Sir Roger Covert about 1249, in Sullington, Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis est née en 1225, à Sullington, Sussex, Angleterre, fille de Richard le Waleys. Elle a épousé Sir Roger Covert environ 1249, à Sullington, Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis wurde 1225 in Sullington, Sussex, England, als Tochter von Richard le Waleys geboren. Sie hat um 1249 in Sullington, Sussex, England Sir Roger Covert geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis è nata il nel 1225 nel Sullington, Sussex, England come figlia di Richard le Waleys. Lei sposato Sir Roger Covert circa 1249, nel Sullington, Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Wallisは1225年にイギリススセックススリントンでRichard le Waleysの娘として生まれた。約1249年にイギリススセックススリントンでSir Roger Covertと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis(은)는 1225년 영국 수세스 설링턴에서 Richard le Waleys의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1249년 영국 수세스 설링턴에서 Sir Roger Covert(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis nasceu em 1225, em Sullington, Sussex, Inglaterra filha de Richard le Waleys. Ela casou-se com Sir Roger Covert aproximadamente 1249, em Sullington, Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos e 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis родилась в 1225 г. в местности суллингтон, суссекс, Англия. Её отца звали Richard le Waleys. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Roger Covert около 1249 г. в местности суллингтон, суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Alice Wallis nació en 1225, en Sullington, Sussex, Inglaterra como hija de Richard le Waleys. Se casó con Sir Roger Covert aproximadamente 1249, en Sullington, Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija.
